visa_types.name: Short Term (Schengen) visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased it yet, please purchase online (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=Russia&utm_campaign=Slovakia%2FRussia&utm_content=English) or at VAC.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[svk > rus > en > Table:Short Term (Schengen) visa > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Russian Rubles in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 35 Euro (inclusive of VAT) payable in Russian Rubles: cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate. 
The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
visa_types.processing_time: From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 calendar days to process the visa application. According to the Article 23 of REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL establishing a Community code on Visas (Visa Code), the period of consideration of your visa application by respective Consular authority may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.
visa_types.forms:
Application form for Short Term visas – English
Application form for Short Term visas – Russian
Application form for Short Term visas – Slovakian
Consent form
Dear applicants, as per the Russian Federal Law on Personal Data Protection, all applicants need to ensure that the consent form is signed and submitted along with each application. Please pay attention that the form is in Russian language only.
Consent Form
To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for a short term (up to 90 days in any 180 days). Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This category of visa caters to people wishing to visit Slovakia for a short term (up to 90 days in any 180 days). Applicants will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## Visa Fees

[svk > rus > en > Table:Short Term (Schengen) visa > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Russian Rubles in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 35 Euro (inclusive of VAT) payable in Russian Rubles: cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate. 
The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased it yet, please purchase online (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=Russia&utm_campaign=Slovakia%2FRussia&utm_content=English) or at VAC.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph 35mm x 45mm (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes 15 calendar days to process the visa application. According to the Article 23 of REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL establishing a Community code on Visas (Visa Code), the period of consideration of your visa application by respective Consular authority may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Application form for Short Term visas – English
Application form for Short Term visas – Russian
Application form for Short Term visas – Slovakian
Consent form
Dear applicants, as per the Russian Federal Law on Personal Data Protection, all applicants need to ensure that the consent form is signed and submitted along with each application. Please pay attention that the form is in Russian language only.
Consent Form
To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A national visa is the permission granted by the Slovak Republic authorising its holder for the stay in the territory of the Slovak Republic for the duration of the validity of the visa and in other Member States for a period not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period under the conditions stipulated in the Schengen Borders Code. The national visa may be issued with maximum validity of 1 year.
This visa type suits to an applicant who is going to reunify with a close relative resides in the Slovak Republic, but not a Slovak citizen.
The conditions of granting of the national visa are regulated by the Act No. 404/2011 Coll. on Residence of Foreigners and on the Amendment and Supplementation to Certain Acts.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A national visa is the permission granted by the Slovak Republic authorising its holder for the stay in the territory of the Slovak Republic for the duration of the validity of the visa and in other Member States for a period not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period under the conditions stipulated in the Schengen Borders Code. The national visa may be issued with maximum validity of 1 year.
This visa type suits to an applicant who is going to reunify with a close relative resides in the Slovak Republic, but not a Slovak citizen.
The conditions of granting of the national visa are regulated by the Act No. 404/2011 Coll. on Residence of Foreigners and on the Amendment and Supplementation to Certain Acts.

## Visa Fees

[svk > rus > en > Table:Family Reunification (with a citizen of another country) > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Russian Rubles in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 35 Euro (inclusive of VAT) payable in Russian Rubles: cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for National visa application. If you have not purchased it yet, please purchase online (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=Russia&utm_campaign=Slovakia%2FRussia&utm_content=English) or at VAC.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph 30mmx35mm (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes up to 30 working days to process the visa application. The national visa can be issued after the prior approval of the Ministry of Interior of Slovak Republic only. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Application form for National Visas – English
Application form for National Visas – Russian
Application form for National Visas – Slovakian
Consent form
Dear applicants, as per the Russian Federal Law on Personal Data Protection, all applicants need to ensure that the consent form is signed and submitted along with each application. Please pay attention that the form is in Russian language only.
Consent Form
To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A national visa is the permission granted by the Slovak Republic authorising its holder for the stay in the territory of the Slovak Republic for the duration of the validity of the visa and in other Member States for a period not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period under the conditions stipulated in the Schengen Borders Code. The national visa may be issued with maximum validity of 1 year.
Application for a National D visa for language courses students can be accepted in case the applicant is going to attend the courses at least 25 hours per week and the duration of the course is more than 90 days.
The conditions of granting of the national visa are regulated by the Act No. 404/2011 Coll. on Residence of Foreigners and on the Amendment and Supplementation to Certain Acts.

Application for a National D visa for language courses students can be accepted in case the applicant is going to attend the courses at least 25 hours per week and the duration of the course is more than 90 days.
The conditions of granting of the national visa are regulated by the Act No. 404/2011 Coll. on Residence of Foreigners and on the Amendment and Supplementation to Certain Acts.

The conditions of granting of the national visa are regulated by the Act No. 404/2011 Coll. on Residence of Foreigners and on the Amendment and Supplementation to Certain Acts.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A national visa is the permission granted by the Slovak Republic authorising its holder for the stay in the territory of the Slovak Republic for the duration of the validity of the visa and in other Member States for a period not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period under the conditions stipulated in the Schengen Borders Code. The national visa may be issued with maximum validity of 1 year.
Application for a National D visa for language courses students can be accepted in case the applicant is going to attend the courses at least 25 hours per week and the duration of the course is more than 90 days.
The conditions of granting of the national visa are regulated by the Act No. 404/2011 Coll. on Residence of Foreigners and on the Amendment and Supplementation to Certain Acts.

## Visa Fees

[svk > rus > en > Table:National visa – Study > Visa Fees 1]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in Russian Rubles in Cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 35 Euro (inclusive of VAT) payable in Russian Rubles: cash or by Debit/Credit Card.
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Document Checklist – Click here
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased it yet, please purchase online (https://vasco.cherehapa.ru/vs?utm_source=TMI&utm_medium=Russia&utm_campaign=Slovakia%2FRussia&utm_content=English) or at VAC.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph 30mmx35mm (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Please stick the photograph on the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements (click here), then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre. 

## Processing Time
From the date of receipt of application at the Embassy of Slovakia, it takes up to 30 working days to process the visa application. The national visa can be issued after the prior approval of the Ministry of Interior of Slovak Republic only. The duration of days when the application is in transit between the Visa Application Centre and the Embassy are not counted.

## Download Forms
Application form for National Visas – English
Application form for National Visas – Russian
Application form for National Visas – Slovakian
Consent form
Dear applicants, as per the Russian Federal Law on Personal Data Protection, all applicants need to ensure that the consent form is signed and submitted along with each application. Please pay attention that the form is in Russian language only.
Consent Form
To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
